commands.repair_reason: - [P1] The implementation has no discrete repair finding, but the external contributor must make the latest-head real behavior proof reproducible before a human merge decision.; Cleared: The narrow rewrite remains subject to HTTP(S) protocol validation and the existing SSRF-guarded fetch path, with no dependency, permission, credential, or supply-chain changes. (sha=ad5933f0522ef7e2d7f700f22f78b32fd12f1df3)
commands.review_summary:
The PR adds scheme-adjacent whitespace recovery to `web_fetch`, propagates the parsed canonical URL through guarded and provider fetch paths, and adds three URL-normalization regression tests.

PR surface: Source +9, Tests +53. Total +62 across 2 files.

Reproducibility: yes. from source with high confidence: current main sends the reported scheme-adjacent whitespace directly to `new URL()` and fails before transport. This read-only review did not execute the tool locally.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] The implementation has no discrete repair finding, but the external contributor must make the latest-head real behavior proof reproducible before a human merge decision.

target.title: fix(web_fetch): strip whitespace from URLs to prevent new URL() parse errors
target.body:
## Summary

Fixes #91651: LLMs occasionally generate `web_fetch` URLs with accidental spaces (e.g. `"https:// docs.example.com/page"`) which cause `new URL()` to throw `Invalid URL: must be http or https`.

**Revised approach** (per ClawSweeper review): Instead of unconditionally stripping all whitespace, the fix uses try-catch fallback:
1. Try `new URL(params.url)` first — preserves standard percent-encoding for valid URLs
2. Only if parsing fails, fall back to whitespace-stripped URL — recovers from LLM-generated space typos
3. This ensures valid URLs with path/query spaces (e.g. `https://example.com/a%20b`) keep their percent-encoding instead of being silently rewritten

## Linked context

Closes #91651

## Real behavior proof (required for external PRs)

- **Behavior or issue addressed:** `web_fetch` tool rejects URLs with accidental internal whitespace (LLM generation artifact). Old approach was too aggressive (stripped all whitespace, breaking valid URLs). New try-catch approach preserves valid URL semantics.
- **Real environment tested:** macOS, Node v22.22.3, latest upstream/main (050c0813b)
- **Exact steps or command run after this patch:**

```bash
node proof-91693.mjs
```

- **Evidence after fix (copied live output):**

```
=== URL Parsing Fix: try-catch fallback ===

Case 1: LLM-generated whitespace
  Input:  "https:// docs.example.com/page"
  Raw URL() throws: true
  Recovered href: https://docs.example.com/page
  PASS: true

Case 2: Tab after scheme
  Input:  "https://\texample.com/path"
  Recovered href: https://example.com/path
  PASS: true

Case 3: Valid URL with path/query spaces
  Input:  "https://example.com/a b/search?q=hello world"
  Raw URL() succeeds: true
  Result href: https://example.com/a%20b/search?q=hello%20world
  Path spaces encoded: true  Query spaces encoded: true
  PASS: true (spaces preserved as %20, NOT stripped)

Case 4: Normal URL (no-op)
  Input:  "https://example.com/normal"
  Result href: https://example.com/normal
  PASS: true

=== Live HTTP fetch with space-stripped URL ===

Malformed URL: "https:// example.com"
Cleaned URL:   "https://example.com"
HTTP status: 200
Content-length: chunked
Body preview: <!doctype html><html lang="en"><head><title>Example Domain</title>...
Live fetch: PASS

=== URL parsing: ALL PASSED ===
=== Proof #91693: COMPLETE ===
```

- **Observed result after fix:** Malformed URLs are recovered via whitespace stripping, while valid URLs with path/query spaces retain correct percent-encoding. Live HTTP fetch confirms the cleaned URL resolves.
- **What was not tested:** All HTTP URLs that could be affected by whitespace removal (infinite combinatorial space). The fix uses standard URL parser behavior for valid URLs.
- **Proof limitations or environment constraints:** Live fetch depends on network access; URL parsing logic is deterministic and verified across 4 edge cases.

## Tests and validation

**Which commands did you run?**
```bash
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run src/agents/tools/web-fetch.cf-markdown.test.ts --reporter=verbose
# 9 passed (1 test file, includes new "preserves valid path/query spaces as percent-encoding" test)
```

**What regression coverage was added or updated?**
- `"recovers from URLs with accidental internal whitespace (#91651)"` — fetches URL with space after scheme, verifies cleanup
- `"preserves valid path/query spaces as percent-encoding"` — verifies valid path/query spaces are percent-encoded, NOT stripped

## Risk checklist

**Did user-visible behavior change?** Yes — URLs with internal whitespace now succeed instead of throwing errors, while valid URLs with path/query spaces are unchanged.
**Did config, environment, or migration behavior change?** No
**Did security, auth, secrets, network, or tool execution behavior change?** No
**What is the highest-risk area?** Try-catch fallback could mask different URL parse errors. Mitigation: second parse only strips whitespace (same operation as before), and SSRF guard still validates the parsed URL.
**How is that risk mitigated?** Protocol check and SSRF validation remain after parsed URL is obtained. Invalid URLs that are not fixed by whitespace removal still throw.

## Current review state

**What is the next action?** Requesting ClawSweeper re-review.
**What is still waiting on author, maintainer, CI, or external proof?** None — proof updated with live URL parsing demo + real HTTP fetch.

---

Co-Authored-By: Claude Code <noreply@anthropic.com>
